# Psalms 7

## Verses (17)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | O Lord my God, I put my faith in you; take me out of the hands of him who is cruel to me, and make me free; | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | So that he may not come rushing on my soul like a lion, wounding it, while there is no one to be my saviour. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | O Lord my God, if I have done this; if my hands have done any wrong; | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | If I have given back evil to him who did evil to me, or have taken anything from him who was against me without cause; | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | Let my hater go after my soul and take it; let my life be crushed to the earth, and my honour into the dust. (Selah.) | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | Come up, Lord, in your wrath; be lifted up against my haters; be awake, my God, give orders for the judging. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | The meeting of the nations will be round you; take your seat, then, over them, on high. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | The Lord will be judge of the peoples; give a decision for me, O Lord, because of my righteousness, and let my virtue have its reward. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | O let the evil of the evil-doer come to an end, but give strength to the upright: for men's minds and hearts are tested by the God of righteousness. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | God, who is the saviour of the upright in heart, is my breastplate. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | God is the judge of the upright, and is angry with the evil-doers every day. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | If a man is not turned from his evil, he will make his sword sharp; his bow is bent and ready. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | He has made ready for him the instruments of death; he makes his arrows flames of fire. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | That man is a worker of evil; the seed of wrongdoing has given birth to deceit. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | He has made a hole deep in the earth, and is falling into the hole which he has made | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | His wrongdoing will come back to him, and his violent behaviour will come down on his head. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | I will give praise to the Lord for his righteousness; I will make a song to the name of the Lord Most High. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
